attempts.prompt:
You are a senior engineer reviewing for FEASIBILITY IN 24 HOURS WITH 4 PEOPLE.
Target hardware: MacBook M3 Pro (36GB class), MSI Stealth 16 AI+ (Intel Core Ultra 9 386H, 50 TOPS NPU).
Available OSS: Ollama, MLX, llama.cpp, ONNX Runtime/DirectML, Qdrant Edge, whisper.cpp/mlx-whisper, Piper, Kokoro.

Idea:
---
Airgap Incident Copilot: on-device copilot for field emergency responders. Offline STT (mlx-whisper) + Gemma 3n E4B + Qdrant Edge with pre-loaded safety protocols + Piper TTS. Voice-first. Works in airplane mode. Sync when reconnected.
---

Respond with plain text in this shape:

LANDS_IN_24H:
- <bullet>
- <bullet>

SKIP_OR_SWAP:
- <thing that probably doesn't + cheaper alternative>

LATENCY_GUESS_M3PRO: <end-to-end estimate in ms with one-line reasoning>

HIDDEN_DEPENDENCY: <single biggest hidden dependency>
attempts.response:
LANDS_IN_24H:
- Voice pipeline integration (mlx-whisper → Gemma 3n E4B via MLX → Piper TTS) can be wired up in a day
- Qdrant Edge with pre-loaded safety protocol embeddings is straightforward vector DB setup
- Basic voice-first UI with push-to-talk and audio playback is minimal viable interface
- Sync mechanism when reconnected is just basic file/state upload to cloud endpoint

SKIP_OR_SWAP:
- Skip custom wake word detection + use simple push-to-talk button instead
- Skip complex conversation memory + use stateless query/response for MVP
- Swap Piper for macOS built-in TTS initially (one less integration point)

LATENCY_GUESS_M3PRO: 3000-4000ms end-to-end (whisper transcription dominates at 2-3s, LLM inference ~500ms, TTS ~500ms)

HIDDEN_DEPENDENCY: Pre-loaded safety protocol dataset needs to be curated, chunked, embedded, and validated - this content preparation could easily consume 50% of development time

attempts.prompt:
You are a latency-obsessed performance engineer.
Target: user-perceived latency < 150ms p50 for the main interaction.

Idea:
---
Airgap Incident Copilot: on-device copilot for field emergency responders. Offline STT (mlx-whisper) + Gemma 3n E4B + Qdrant Edge with pre-loaded safety protocols + Piper TTS. Voice-first. Works in airplane mode. Sync when reconnected.
---

Respond with plain text in this shape:

CRITICAL_PATH: <first-token time, total time, breakdown>

MEM_BOUND_VS_COMPUTE_BOUND:
- Apple Silicon: <which steps>
- Intel+NPU: <which steps>

TOP_3_OPTIMIZATIONS:
1. <change + expected delta, e.g. "Q4_K_M quant: -35% TTFT">
2.
3.

WHAT_TO_MEASURE: <metrics the bench must record to validate above>
attempts.response:
CRITICAL_PATH: STT (80-120ms) → Intent parsing + retrieval (20-30ms) → LLM first token (40-80ms) → TTS audio start (10-20ms). Total: 150-250ms, target violated by STT + LLM TTFT.

MEM_BOUND_VS_COMPUTE_BOUND:
- Apple Silicon: STT compute-bound on ANE, LLM memory-bound (bandwidth), TTS compute-bound, Qdrant memory-bound
- Intel+NPU: STT compute-bound on NPU, LLM severely memory-bound (DDR5 bandwidth), TTS mixed, Qdrant memory-bound

TOP_3_OPTIMIZATIONS:
1. Streaming STT with 200ms chunks + speculative LLM start: -60ms TTFT
2. Gemma 3B → 1B with Q4_K_M, prefill optimization: -25ms first token  
3. Qdrant → in-memory hashmap for <1000 protocols: -15ms retrieval

WHAT_TO_MEASURE: Voice activity detection to STT start, STT chunk-by-chunk latency, LLM prefill vs decode time, memory bandwidth utilization, TTS synthesis start time, end-to-end voice-to-audio latency with percentiles.

attempts.prompt:
You are a privacy & security skeptic. You look for hidden cloud dependencies.

Idea:
---
Airgap Incident Copilot: on-device copilot for field emergency responders. Offline STT (mlx-whisper) + Gemma 3n E4B + Qdrant Edge with pre-loaded safety protocols + Piper TTS. Voice-first. Works in airplane mode. Sync when reconnected.
---

Respond with plain text in this shape:

NETWORK_CALLS_IF_UNCHANGED:
- <feature>: <endpoint>
- ...

CAN_BE_ELIMINATED:
- <list>

CAN_BE_GATED_OFFLINE_MODE:
- <list>

HOW_TO_PROVE_ZERO_EGRESS_LIVE: <one practical method — tcpdump, Little Snitch, or equivalent>
attempts.response:
NETWORK_CALLS_IF_UNCHANGED:
- MLX-Whisper model updates: huggingface.co/mlx-community/whisper-*
- Gemma 3 model downloads: huggingface.co/google/gemma-* 
- Qdrant dependency updates: github.com/qdrant/qdrant
- Piper TTS voice models: github.com/rhasspy/piper/releases
- System telemetry: Apple/Google analytics endpoints
- Certificate validation: OCSP responders
- NTP time sync: pool.ntp.org
- DNS queries for any hardcoded domains
- Package manager checks: pip/conda/homebrew update servers
- Crash reporting: Sentry, Bugsnag, or similar
- License validation: vendor license servers

CAN_BE_ELIMINATED:
- All telemetry/analytics calls
- Automatic model updates 
- Crash reporting to external services
- Usage statistics collection
- Performance metrics uploading
- License phone-home checks
- Automatic dependency updates

CAN_BE_GATED_OFFLINE_MODE:
- Model downloads (pre-bundle instead)
- Safety protocol database updates
- Sync functionality when reconnected
- Software update checks
- Certificate revocation checks
- Time synchronization

HOW_TO_PROVE_ZERO_EGRESS_LIVE: Use Little Snitch in silent mode with logging enabled, run the app through all functions for 24+ hours in airplane mode, then analyze the connection attempt logs to verify zero outbound requests beyond OS-level system calls.

attempts.prompt:
You are a hackathon-judge veteran thinking about LIVE DEMO RISK on stage.

Idea:
---
Airgap Incident Copilot: on-device copilot for field emergency responders. Offline STT (mlx-whisper) + Gemma 3n E4B + Qdrant Edge with pre-loaded safety protocols + Piper TTS. Voice-first. Works in airplane mode. Sync when reconnected.
---

Respond with plain text in this shape:

TOP_3_FAILURE_MODES:
1. <mode>
2.
3.

TOP_3_MEMORABLE_MOMENTS:
1. <moment>
2.
3.

GO_NOGO: <YES | WITH CHANGES | NO>
IF_WITH_CHANGES: <exactly which changes; else "N/A">
attempts.response:
TOP_3_FAILURE_MODES:
1. Offline STT fails to recognize speech in noisy demo environment or with presenter's accent/speaking style, leading to garbled or no transcription
2. Local LLM (Gemma 3n) generates slow, irrelevant, or inappropriate emergency responses that make the system look unreliable for critical situations
3. Audio pipeline breaks between STT → LLM → TTS, resulting in long delays, audio dropouts, or robotic/unintelligible speech output

TOP_3_MEMORABLE_MOMENTS:
1. Demonstrating true airplane mode functionality by dramatically disconnecting all networks mid-demo and showing system still works flawlessly
2. Simulating realistic emergency scenario with audio chaos (sirens, shouting) and showing the system correctly identifies protocols and responds clearly
3. Voice interaction flow working so naturally that it feels like talking to an expert human emergency coordinator rather than AI

GO_NOGO: WITH CHANGES

IF_WITH_CHANGES: Pre-record backup audio samples of successful interactions, have slide deck with screenshots of working system as fallback, test extensively with demo laptop's specific microphone/speakers beforehand, prepare manual text input method if voice fails, and have a simple "golden path" demo script that avoids complex emergency scenarios that could generate problematic AI responses

Respond with plain text in this shape:

VERDICT: GO | GO-WITH-CHANGES | NO-GO

TOP_3_MUST_DO_CHANGES:
1.
2.
3.

1.
2.
3.

attempts.response:
VERDICT: GO-WITH-CHANGES

TOP_3_MUST_DO_CHANGES:
1. Pre-record backup audio samples for demo failsafe and test extensively with actual demo hardware's microphone/speakers to avoid audio pipeline failures
2. Curate and validate safety protocol dataset first (allocate 12+ hours for content prep since this is the hidden 50% time sink)
3. Implement streaming STT with 200ms chunks + speculative LLM start to get closer to acceptable latency (target sub-300ms TTFT)

TOP_3_BENCHMARK_SCENARIOS:
1. Airplane mode test: Full voice interaction cycle (question → protocol retrieval → spoken response) with zero network connectivity in <4 seconds end-to-end
2. Noisy environment test: Accurate speech recognition and clear protocol guidance with background noise at 65+ dB
3. Protocol coverage test: Successful retrieval and relevant response for 20+ different emergency scenarios from pre-loaded safety database

PITCH_READY_CLAIM: "Complete emergency protocol guidance in under 4 seconds, fully offline, with zero data leaving your device - demonstrated live in airplane mode"

- Usage statistics collection
- Performance metrics uploading
- License phone-home checks
- Automatic dependency updates

CAN_BE_GATED_OFFLINE_MODE:
- Model downloads (pre-bundle instead)
- Safety protocol database updates
- Sync functionality when reconnected
- Software update checks
- Certificate revocation checks
- Time synchronization

HOW_TO_PROVE_ZERO_EGRESS_LIVE: Use Little Snitch in silent mode with logging enabled, run the app through all functions for 24+ hours in airplane mode, then analyze the connection attempt logs to verify zero outbound requests beyond OS-level system calls.
inputSnapshot.reviewer-demo-risk:
TOP_3_FAILURE_MODES:
1. Offline STT fails to recognize speech in noisy demo environment or with presenter's accent/speaking style, leading to garbled or no transcription
2. Local LLM (Gemma 3n) generates slow, irrelevant, or inappropriate emergency responses that make the system look unreliable for critical situations
3. Audio pipeline breaks between STT → LLM → TTS, resulting in long delays, audio dropouts, or robotic/unintelligible speech output

TOP_3_MEMORABLE_MOMENTS:
1. Demonstrating true airplane mode functionality by dramatically disconnecting all networks mid-demo and showing system still works flawlessly
2. Simulating realistic emergency scenario with audio chaos (sirens, shouting) and showing the system correctly identifies protocols and responds clearly
3. Voice interaction flow working so naturally that it feels like talking to an expert human emergency coordinator rather than AI

GO_NOGO: WITH CHANGES

IF_WITH_CHANGES: Pre-record backup audio samples of successful interactions, have slide deck with screenshots of working system as fallback, test extensively with demo laptop's specific microphone/speakers beforehand, prepare manual text input method if voice fails, and have a simple "golden path" demo script that avoids complex emergency scenarios that could generate problematic AI responses

steps.outputSnapshot:
VERDICT: GO-WITH-CHANGES

TOP_3_MUST_DO_CHANGES:
1. Pre-record backup audio samples for demo failsafe and test extensively with actual demo hardware's microphone/speakers to avoid audio pipeline failures
2. Curate and validate safety protocol dataset first (allocate 12+ hours for content prep since this is the hidden 50% time sink)
3. Implement streaming STT with 200ms chunks + speculative LLM start to get closer to acceptable latency (target sub-300ms TTFT)

TOP_3_BENCHMARK_SCENARIOS:
1. Airplane mode test: Full voice interaction cycle (question → protocol retrieval → spoken response) with zero network connectivity in <4 seconds end-to-end
2. Noisy environment test: Accurate speech recognition and clear protocol guidance with background noise at 65+ dB
3. Protocol coverage test: Successful retrieval and relevant response for 20+ different emergency scenarios from pre-loaded safety database

PITCH_READY_CLAIM: "Complete emergency protocol guidance in under 4 seconds, fully offline, with zero data leaving your device - demonstrated live in airplane mode"
